messages.content:
Assignment
You are working for a medium sized IT services company developing products and
providing services for the clients which is expected to contribute to the growth of the
company.
DevSphere Services Inc.
DevSphere Services Inc. is a medium sized multi-national company having its
development centres in USA and Nepal. The administrative departments: Finance,
Human Resources Management, and Marketing are based at the Corporate Office in
Florida, USA.
Nepal is the Development centre and has its local president along with a pool of
Managers and other required Development Team members.
Board
Kevin Brown Chief Executive Officer
Robert Smith Chief Operating Officer
Brad Kasper Chief Financial Officer
Julia Stephenson Chief HR officer
Richard Wilson Chief Technology Officer
Howles Buffet President – USA
Kedar Basnet President - Nepal
Kyle Jones Director of Project Management
Helen Peterson Director of Sales and Marketing
Each country, region has their own management, engineering team. The company
works on multiple projects as per the requirements from different clients. The company
has just signed a contract with a new client (a Multiplex company) to create a software
application for them - “Show Time”, a mobile app that allows users for easy booking of
the movie tickets.
Functional Requirements for “Show Time” app
This app has several features to provide users with booking the movie ticket
experience, and provide the admin the control over the data. Features of the app is
listed out below:
User Panel:
● Registration and Log-in: User can provide details such as name, email, phone
number and password, a user can register themselves on the app. Using the
credentials, a user can log-in within the app.
● Show Listings: User can optimally showcase a list of shows right from show
category to show name, venue & location, offers, ratings, as well as booking
charges. Users should be given with the ability to search something they are
looking perhaps with a type-ahead style search box.
● Show Detail: Right after selecting a desired movie or event, the user gets
directed to further details such as event/show description like name, images, and
duration and later with data like different venues with date and time, customer
satisfaction ratings and reviews, and more information.
● Show Booking: Provide Book Ticket icon as a feature that re-direct users to a
particular cinema, show list, show timings, graphical representation of seat
arrangements to select the desired number of seat easily and then book.
● Booking Screen: Users can view the order summary, which may consist of: Show
Name; Image, Date; Time, Venue Name, Ticket Price, Seat Number, Total Price,
View and confirm Email ID/Phone Number.
● Payment Options: Through this feature, users can make payments seamlessly
through a number of channels.
● Reviews and Rating: User can rate, review and share their feedback.
Admin Panel:
● Data Control: With the help of a Dashboard, an admin can look and control over
all the data that is taking place within the application. Be it user list, active users,
add new users or may edit/remove users. This panel also comprises all the
details and number of listed cinemas, movies, total booking, total earning, and so
much more. From this place, an admin can also manage shows and venues.
Non-Functional Requirements
● The platform’s website should handle 100,000 users at a time without affecting its
performance
● Should support on iOS and Android platform
● Maximum 5 seconds of response time
● Disaster recovery
You need to carry out this project, for which you are required to perform the tasks as
listed below.

Task 1: Project Initiation
1.1 Define Project Scope
1.2 Identify Stakeholders
1.3 Define Project Objectives and Success Criteria
1.4 Prepare Project Initiation Document (PID)

Task 2: Project Planning
2.1 Create Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)
2.2 Develop a detailed project schedule
2.3 Estimate project costs and resources
2.4 Develop a risk management plan
2.5 Develop a communication plan
2.6 Develop a project quality plan

Task 3: Project Execution
3.1 Assemble project team
3.2 Conduct project team meeting and assign tasks
3.3 Develop the “Show Time” app according to the defined functional and non-functional requirements
3.4 Monitor progress against project schedule
3.5 Ensure effective communication among team members
3.6 Resolve issues and manage risks as they arise

Task 4: Quality Assurance and Testing
4.1 Develop a test plan and test cases
4.2 Perform functional and non-functional testing
4.3 Conduct performance testing to ensure the app can handle 100,000 users without affecting performance
4.4 Perform user acceptance testing with stakeholders
4.5 Address and resolve any defects or issues identified during testing

Task 5: Deployment
5.1 Develop a deployment plan
5.2 Setup necessary infrastructure and environments for deployment
5.3 Train client's staff on using the “Show Time” app
5.4 Deploy the app on iOS and Android platforms
5.5 Launch the app and promote it to potential users

Task 6: Project Closure
6.1 Conduct a project review meeting
6.2 Document lessons learned
6.3 Obtain project sign-off from the client
6.4 Prepare and submit a project closure report
6.5 Celebrate project success with the team

Task 7: Post-Project Support and Maintenance
7.1 Provide ongoing support to the client
7.2 Monitor and address any post-deployment issues
7.3 Conduct periodic updates and improvements to the app
7.4 Maintain a continued relationship with the client

Consider the various approaches and system/software development methods
that might be appropriate for this particular firm and developing this particular
system. Explain in outline two substantially different methods – and compare
their suitability for this case. Make recommendations in relation to this specific
case – giving a full rational.

Approach 1: Agile Software Development Method

Agile is a widely popular software development method that focuses on continuous improvement, customer feedback, and iterative progress. In Agile, the development process is divided into several smaller sprints, usually lasting two to four weeks. Each sprint consists of a specific workload, and the project team works together to complete that workload in the given timeframe. At the end of each sprint, the team presents a functional demo of the project to the stakeholders, who provide feedback for further improvements.

Pros:
- Encourages communication and collaboration among team members and stakeholders.
- Continuously incorporating customer feedback ensures better alignment with the client's requirements.
- Flexibility to respond to changing requirements and priorities.
- Faster delivery of the product with the help of rapid iterative sprints.

Cons:
- The flexibility in the process might lead to scope creep and could affect the project schedule and estimated costs.
- The Agile method requires considerable time and resource investment for continuous involvement and communication between team members and stakeholders.

Approach 2: Waterfall Software Development Method

The Waterfall method is a traditional and sequential software development process in which the project progresses through a series of distinct phases, such as requirements analysis, design, development, testing, deployment, and maintenance. Each phase must be completed before moving on to the next one, with no overlap or iteration between the phases.

Pros:
- Well-structured and easily manageable due to clear milestones and deliverables.
- Better control over project scope and easier to predict project timeline and costs.
- Suitable for projects with well-defined and stable requirements.

Cons:
- Limited flexibility to accommodate changes or new requirements once the project has moved past a certain phase.
- Stakeholders might not have a chance to review and give feedback on the system until the end of the project, which might lead to higher rework costs.

Recommendations for this specific case:

Considering the nature of the “Show Time” app and its requirements, the Agile Software Development Method is recommended for this project. The reasons for this recommendation are as follows:

1. The app development process might require frequent changes and improvements based on user feedback, which makes Agile more suitable due to its inherent flexibility.

2. Agile's focus on communication and collaboration will ensure that the development team and stakeholders are closely engaged throughout the project, leading to better understanding and alignment with client requirements.

3. The iterative sprints of Agile development will allow the team to deliver functional pieces of the app to the client at regular intervals, ensuring a faster delivery of the product and ability to react to the market needs more quickly.

In conclusion, the Agile Software Development Method is better suited for this case, as it offers the flexibility and adaptability required for the development of the “Show Time” app. It also enables the project team to stay closely connected with the stakeholders and adapt to their needs on a continuous basis.

In line with the Prince2 methodology, produce a RACI Matrix chart giving a clear
insight into setup of your project team, the chart should clearly identify the
champions and their roles in the project. Useful templates can be found on the
Official PRINCE2 website. (Note: Keeping in mind the case study, you are free to
make assumptions when setting up your team members and their role).

Below is the RACI Matrix chart for the "Show Time" app development project, which provides a clear understanding of the team setup and their roles.

| Role/Responsibility      | Project Sponsor | Project Manager | Development Team | QA Team | UX/UI Designer | Marketing Team | Client |
|--------------------------|-----------------|-----------------|------------------|---------|----------------|----------------|-------|
| Project Charter Approval | R               | A               | C                | C       | C              | C              | C     |
| Project Planning         | C               | R               | C                | C       | C              | C              | I     |
| Requirement Gathering    | C               | R               | A                | C       | A              | C              | C     |
| Design                   | C               | A               | C                | C       | R              | C              | I     |
| Development              | C               | A               | R                | C       | C              | C              | I     |
| Testing                  | C               | A               | C                | R       | C              | C              | I     |
| Deployment               | C               | R               | A                | C       | A              | C              | I     |
| User Training            | C               | A               | A                | C       | C              | C              | C     |
| Project Monitoring       | C               | R               | C                | C       | C              | C              | I     |
| Marketing & Promotion    | C               | C               | C                | C       | C              | R              | I     |
| Project Closure/Sign-off | R               | A               | C                | C       | C              | C              | C     |

**RACI Legend**
- R: Responsible - The individual who does the work to complete the task
- A: Accountable - The person responsible for ultimately approving the work and ensuring that it is completed correctly
- C: Consulted - The individual who provides input and feedback during the task execution
- I: Informed - The person who needs to be informed about the progress and outcome of the task

Assumptions made for the team setup:
- The project team structure consists of a Project Sponsor, Project Manager, Development Team, QA Team, UX/UI Designer, Marketing Team, and Client.
- The Project Sponsor in this case could be the Director of Project Management, and the Project Manager reports to them.
- Development Team, QA Team, and UX/UI Designer roles are filled by the in-house resources from DevSphere Services Inc.
- Marketing Team is responsible for the promotion and marketing of the "Show Time" app.
- Client plays an essential role in providing feedback and approving the work as it progresses.

Produce an outline plan for the development on the basis of your chosen system
development method or methodology with justification of plan following the
selected methodology

As we have selected the Agile Software Development Method for this "Show Time" app development project, the outline plan is as follows:

1. Project Initiation
- Define the project vision and scope, ensuring that the objectives align with the client's requirements.
- Identify stakeholders and their expectations.
- Gather high-level requirements and establish a product backlog.
- Prepare a project charter, highlighting key objectives, scope, stakeholders, and success criteria.

2. Sprint Planning
- Prioritize the product backlog based on client input, business value, and technical feasibility.
- Divide the project into small, manageable sprints (2-4 weeks).
- Plan the first sprint by selecting the most critical user stories or features from the product backlog.
- Identify specific tasks within each user story and estimate the effort required for each task.

3. Sprint Execution
- Develop the selected user stories or features during the sprint, promoting collaboration among team members.
- Conduct Daily Stand-up meetings (15-minute time-boxed) to discuss progress, updates, and any potential issues.
- Continuously integrate and test the developed features to ensure high-quality output.
- Manage any changes and reprioritize the backlog as necessary.

4. Sprint Review
- At the end of the sprint, showcase the completed features to the client and stakeholders for feedback and validation.
- Assess the sprint performance, highlighting accomplished user stories, and any pending or changed items.

5. Sprint Retrospective
- Conduct a retrospective meeting to identify areas for improvement, lessons learned, and any adjustments required in the process.
- Discuss successes, challenges, and improvement actions to be applied in future sprints.

6. Repeat Steps 2-5
- Repeat the sprint planning, execution, review, and retrospective steps for additional sprints until the entire product backlog is developed, and all features are completed.

7. User Acceptance Testing
- Once all sprints are complete, conduct User Acceptance Testing (UAT) with the client to validate that the developed app meets their requirements.
- Address any feedback or issues found during the UAT.

8. Deployment and Release
- Prepare the app for deployment on iOS and Android platforms.
- Train the client's staff (if necessary) to use and manage the app effectively.
- Release the app to the app stores and promote it to potential users.

9. Post-Release Support & Maintenance
- Provide ongoing support and maintenance to ensure the app performs optimally and caters to any additional feedback or requirements.
- Continuously improve the app through feature enhancements and updates.

Justification for adopting the Agile Software Development Method:
- The Agile method allows for continuous feedback and improvement throughout the development process, ensuring better alignment with the client's requirements.
- The iterative sprints promote faster delivery of functional features, allowing the client to experience the app incrementally.
- It fosters a collaborative environment among team members, enabling them to adapt to changes quickly and efficiently throughout the project lifecycle.
- The method's flexibility accommodates changing user requirements, prioritizing client satisfaction and delivering a high-quality product.
provide justification for each outline plan following selected methodology

Here are the justifications for each stage of the outline plan, highlighting how they align with the Agile Software Development Method:

1. Project Initiation
- Agile methodology emphasizes collaboration with stakeholders and defining a clear project vision. This stage helps align the team and stakeholders towards common goals and sets the stage for iterative development.

2. Sprint Planning
- Agile divides the project into smaller, incremental sprints. Sprint planning is essential to prioritize work based on stakeholder inputs and business value, enabling quick delivery of high-priority features.

3. Sprint Execution
- In the Agile method, the focus is on continuous development, integration, and testing during each sprint. Active collaboration among team members during sprint execution helps build high-quality features and fosters communication.

4. Sprint Review
- Agile encourages continuous stakeholder engagement and feedback, which is achieved through sprint reviews. By showcasing the completed features, teams can ensure that they're on the right track and address any changes or improvements necessary promptly.

5. Sprint Retrospective
- Continuous improvement is a critical aspect of Agile, and sprint retrospectives help in achieving this goal. Reflecting on the team's performance, challenges, and improvement opportunities promotes iterative growth throughout the project.

6. Repeat Steps 2-5
- Following Agile principles, development should progress iteratively, addressing high-priority features first, and continuously refining the product based on stakeholder input. Repeating sprint planning, execution, review, and retrospective helps to ensure a value-driven and adaptive development process.

7. User Acceptance Testing
- As Agile focuses on delivering features that satisfy client requirements, conducting User Acceptance Testing ensures that the application meets the client's expectations and functions according to their needs.

8. Deployment and Release
- Agile's quick delivery approach aligns with releasing the complete application into the public domain, ensuring the client can start benefiting from their investment earlier in the development cycle.

9. Post-Release Support & Maintenance
- Agile's emphasis on continuous improvement and adaptation extends to the support and maintenance phase. By offering post-release support, the development team can address any issues, feedback, or improvements necessary, ensuring the application evolves with the client's needs.

In conclusion, the outlined plan adheres to key Agile principles such as iterative development, continuous improvement, stakeholder collaboration, and adaptive planning. By following this methodology, the project team can ensure they efficiently deliver a high-quality product that addresses the client's requirements and priorities.

Using relevant PRINCE2 templates produce a Project Brief which provides a
foundation for the initiation of the project. Useful templates can be found via the
Official PRINCE2 website.

**Project Brief - Show Time App Development Project**

1. Project Definition

1.1 Project Title: Show Time App Development Project

1.2 Project Background:
DevSphere Services Inc. has signed a contract with a Multiplex company to create the “Show Time” app, a mobile application for booking movie tickets.

1.3 Project Objectives:
- Develop a user-friendly mobile app for iOS and Android devices to provide a seamless movie ticket booking experience.
- Enable users to view movie listings, details, and book tickets on the app.
- Offer secure payment options and incorporate features for rating and reviewing movies.
- Ensure that the app can handle 100,000 users at a time without degrading its performance.

1.4 Desired Outcomes:
- Increased satisfaction among Multiplex company's customers, leading to positive reviews, and higher customer retention.
- Improved ticket sales due to the seamless booking and payment process offered by the app.
- Enhanced brand reputation and market presence for the Multiplex company.

2. Project Scope

2.1 In Scope:
- App development for iOS and Android platforms.
- Integrating various features for user registration, show listings, booking, payments, and reviews.
- Ensuring platform support for high traffic and quick response times.
- Deployment and post-release maintenance.

2.2 Out of Scope:
- Any compliance or localization requirements outside of the original project scope.
- Marketing or promotional activities for the app.

3. Project Constraints & Assumptions

3.1 Constraints:
- Budget: The project must be completed within the allocated budget.
- Time: The app must be developed and released within the agreed-upon timeline.
- Resources: The project team must have adequate resources to complete the project successfully.

3.2 Assumptions:
- The client will provide timely feedback and approvals throughout the project lifecycle.
- The development team has the required expertise and skillsets to complete the project.

4. Project Approach & Methodology

The Agile Software Development Method will be used for this project. This methodology allows for flexibility, iterative development, and continuous feedback from stakeholders throughout the project lifecycle. Key milestones will be defined through project initiation, sprint planning, sprint execution, sprint review, and post-release support.

5. Project Organization & Roles

5.1 Project Board:
- Project Sponsor: Director of Project Management
- Project Manager: Appointed from DevSphere Services Inc.

5.2 Project Team:
- Development Team: DevSphere Services Inc. developers
- QA Team: DevSphere Services Inc. QA professionals
- UX/UI Designer: DevSphere Services Inc. designer

6. Project Timelines & Stages

6.1 Estimated Timeline: 6 months

6.2 Key Stages:
- Project Initiation: 2 weeks
- Sprint Planning: 1 week per sprint
- Sprint Execution: 2-4 weeks per sprint (6-8 sprints)
- Sprint Review and Retrospective: At the end of each sprint
- User Acceptance Testing: 2 weeks
- Deployment and Release: 1 week
- Post-release Support & Maintenance: Ongoing after release

7. Project Deliverables

- Completed "Show Time" app for iOS and Android.
- User manual and documentation.
- UAT test results and validation.
- Project closure report and lessons learned.

8. Required Resources & Budget

- App developers, QA testers, and UX/UI designer.
- Hardware and software resources (development environments, testing tools, etc.).
- Estimated budget allocation for project completion.

9. Project Risks

Potential risks include:
- Requirement changes during the project lifecycle.
- Resource limitations or unavailability.
- Technical challenges during the development, testing, or deployment of the app.

A risk management plan will be developed and maintained throughout the project lifecycle, addressing these and other risks encountered during the project.

Outline the subsequent stages the work will go through – taking into account the
requirements of PRINCE2 and your chosen System/Software Development
method – and relating these to the specific case study scenario.

Following the PRINCE2 methodology and the Agile Software Development method, the subsequent stages for the "Show Time" app development project are as follows:

1. Starting Up a Project (SU)
- Create a Project Brief (completed in the previous response).
- Identify the project team and set up their roles and responsibilities.
- Develop a high-level Project Plan, including objectives, scope, risks, and resource requirements.

2. Initiating a Project (IP)
- Prepare a Project Initiation Document (PID) that outlines the project approach, management, and control structure, resource and budget plans, communication plan, and risk management approach.
- Develop a detailed project plan, highlighting individual sprints, and the scope of each sprint in alignment with the Agile methodology.

3. Directing a Project (DP)
- The Project Board ensures that the project stays within the defined scope, budget, and timeline.
- Implement an effective decision-making process for the project.
- Ensure regular communication between the project team and stakeholders.

4. Managing Stage Boundaries (SB)
- Review the progress of sprints and overall project performance at the end of each sprint, as per the Agile methodology. 
- Communicate project status to the Project Board and other stakeholders.
- Obtain necessary approvals to proceed to the next sprint or project stage.

5. Controlling a Stage (CS)
- Monitor and control the project progress throughout each sprint, implementing the Agile approach for iterative development, integration, and testing.
- Address potential risks and issues, and report them to the Project Board.
- Ensure efficient resource management, communication, and collaboration among team members.

6. Managing Product Delivery (MP)
- Coordinate the delivery of the product, including sprint reviews with the client to validate completed features and gather feedback.
- Conduct User Acceptance Testing (UAT) to ensure the app meets client requirements.
- Deploy the product to the app stores for iOS and Android platforms.

7. Closing a Project (CP)
- Evaluate the project's performance against the defined objectives, timeline, and budget.
- Document lessons learned, project closure report, and celebrate project success with the team.
- Obtain project sign-off from the client and ensure they are satisfied with the product.

By combining the PRINCE2 methodology and Agile Software Development method, we ensure that the "Show Time" app development project is effectively governed, controlled, and guided by iterative and flexible development practices. This approach allows us to deliver a high-quality product that aligns with the client's requirements and adapts to any changes or new requirements throughout the project lifecycle.
Write a brief memorandum on the setting up of a Project Management Team.

Subject: Setting Up a Project Management Team for the Show Time App Development Project

To: [Project Board, Stakeholders, and Project Team]

Cc: [Other Concerned Parties]

Date: [Insert Date]

Dear Team,

As we embark on the development of the "Show Time" app, I'd like to take this opportunity to share our plans for setting up a Project Management Team to ensure the successful execution and delivery of this project. A well-structured and coherent Project Management Team is crucial in achieving efficiency, effective communication, and successful project completion.

1. Project Management Team Composition

The core Project Management Team for the "Show Time" app development project will consist of:

- Project Manager: Responsible for overseeing the project, managing resources, schedules, risks, and ensuring the timely delivery of project outcomes.
- Development Team Lead: Will lead the App Developers, planning and monitoring development tasks, ensuring adherence to technical and quality standards.
- QA Team Lead: Responsible for leading the QA Team, planning and executing testing activities, and maintaining quality throughout the project lifecycle.
- UX/UI Designer: Will focus on creating a user-friendly and visually appealing design that meets the client's expectations and requirements.

2. Communication & Collaboration

Effective communication and collaboration within the Project Management Team are critical to managing tasks, addressing risks and issues, and ensuring on-time delivery within budget constraints. The following communication channels will be established to facilitate a smooth flow of information within the team:

- Regular team meetings: Will be held to discuss updates, issues, and concerns on the project progress, ensuring all team members stay informed and engaged.
- Daily Stand-up meetings: As the project follows Agile methodology, Daily Stand-up meetings will be conducted during Sprint Execution to report progress, next steps, and any impediments on a daily basis, fostering quick resolution of issues.
- Designated communication tools: Platforms such as Slack, Microsoft Teams, or other project management tools will be used for instant communication and collaboration among team members.

3. Training & Skill Development

To ensure the team's effectiveness and efficient handling of project tasks, training sessions may be conducted (if necessary) to familiarize the team with the project domain or technology tools required for the development of the "Show Time" app. These sessions will empower team members and ensure the acquisition of specialized skills pertaining to the project.

4. Roles and Responsibilities

Each member of the Project Management Team will be assigned specific roles and responsibilities (as detailed in the RACI Matrix). Along with the execution of their designated roles, team members are encouraged to collaborate actively and develop a continuous improvement mindset for collective growth and learning.

By setting up a structured and efficient Project Management Team, we aim to ensure a successful project outcome that meets the client's requirements and high-quality standards. As we proceed with the project, let's work together diligently, keeping open lines of communication and collaboration throughout the process.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]

[Your Title & Organization]

Spoken Presentation 
Present an overview to the management based on the report and be prepared to
answer questions. 
The style and format of all documents and the presentation should take into account the
intended reader/listener – i.e. they should be appropriate for the business context.

[Begin Presentation]

Ladies and gentlemen of the management team,

Thank you for joining me today as I present an overview of the upcoming "Show Time" app development project. This presentation will provide you with an understanding of the project’s objectives, scope, development methodology, and timeline.

Our company, DevSphere Services Inc, has been awarded the contract for developing the "Show Time" app, a mobile application that allows users to book movie tickets seamlessly. Our client is a Multiplex company aiming to increase customer satisfaction, ticket sales, and enhance their overall market presence.

The project's primary objectives include:
- Developing a user-friendly mobile app for iOS and Android platforms.
- Enabling users to view, book, and pay for movie tickets on the app.
- Incorporating features for rating, reviewing, and sharing information about movies.
- Ensuring the platform can handle up to 100,000 users simultaneously without affecting performance.

To achieve these objectives, we have chosen the Agile Software Development Method. This approach allows us to accommodate changes in requirements quickly, maintain close collaboration with the client, and deliver functional features incrementally at each sprint.

In line with the PRINCE2 methodology and Agile development approach, we have set up a structured Project Management Team, including:

- Project Manager
- Development Team Lead
- QA Team Lead
- UX/UI Designer

We will be dividing the development process into multiple 2-4 week sprints. At the end of each sprint, completed features will be showcased to the client for feedback and validation. This iterative approach ensures that we deliver a high-quality product that meets the client's expectations and requirements.

The estimated timeline for the project is six months. This timeline includes all stages of the agile development process, from project initiation to user acceptance testing, deployment, and post-release support.

In conclusion, I am confident that by adopting the PRINCE2 methodology and Agile Software Development method and maintaining efficient communication and collaboration among our team members and stakeholders, we will successfully deliver the "Show Time" app in a timely manner.

I will now open the floor for any questions or concerns you may have.

[End Presentation]
